message.content:
Adversarially VERIFY this research claim about how a top deep-research system scores on DeepResearch Bench.

CLAIM: The real SOTA under POLARIS's own judge (GPT-5.5) is cellcog-max at 55.78, not 52.65. POLARIS's true gap is ~0.120, not 0.088.
DETAIL: The benchmark switched its official RACE evaluator from Gemini-2.5-Pro to GPT-5.5 on 11 May 2026 (Gemini-2.5-Pro deprecation), and maintains a SEPARATE data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv. Full GPT-5.5 board: cellcog-max 55.78 (comp 56.34 / insight 57.08 / IF 55.30 / read 51.94), WhaleCloud-DocChain 54.78, bodhi 54.07, lunon 53.51, dalpha 53.10, sourcery 51.17, gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.98, openai-deepresearch 47.84, perplexity 43.05, grok-deeper-search 41.22. Since POLARIS is measured with the gpt-5.5 evaluator, this is the ONLY directly comparable board. Cross-judge scores are NOT comparable and the mapping is nonlinear (compresses the top, lifts the bottom): cellcog 56.67→55.78, gemini-DR 49.71→49.98, openai-DR 46.45→47.84, perplexity 40.46→43.05, grok 38.22→41.22. Target 55.78.
SYSTEM: other
SOURCE GIVEN: https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/raw/main/data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv
STATED CONFIDENCE: high

Go to the source. Check it actually says this. Check it is CURRENT (the benchmark's leaderboard and evaluator both
changed in 2026 — the original paper's numbers are stale; Gemini-2.5-Pro was replaced by GPT-5.5 as the RACE judge).
Default to UNSUPPORTED if you cannot confirm it from a primary source. We would rather have five verified facts
than thirty plausible ones.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
Web search results for query: "DeepResearch Bench leaderboard RACE cellcog-max"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - baidubce/qianfan-deepresearch-bench: Baidu Qianfan Deep Research · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/baidubce/qianfan-deepresearch-bench"},{"title":"GitHub - onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench: Onyx Submission for Deep Research Benchmark · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench"},{"title":"Upload 2 files · muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard at 3e1351b","url":"https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/commit/3e1351b9af80f871ca1a7080002b9d3047ac4012"},{"title":"muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard at main","url":"https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/tree/main"},{"title":"DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents","url":"https://deepresearch-bench.github.io/"},{"title":"GitHub - Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench: DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench"},{"title":"Enterprise Deep Research Agents: A Strategic Analysis | by Tuhin Sharma | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@tuhinsharma121/enterprise-deep-research-agents-a-strategic-analysis-32fb3ee6325d"},{"title":"Orchestrating Specialized Agents for Trustworthy Enterprise RAG","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.18267"},{"title":"Deep Researcher with Sequential Plan Reflection and Candidates Crossover (Deep Researcher Reflect Evolve)","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20843"},{"title":"DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.11763"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the DeepResearch Bench leaderboard, specifically regarding RACE scores and Cellcog:

## DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard - RACE Framework

The RACE (Reference-based Adaptive Criteria-driven Evaluation framework with Dynamic Weighting) evaluates the quality of generated research reports.

### Cellcog Performance

Cellcog is ranked 2nd on the leaderboard with an overall RACE score of 54.54, with component scores of 54.43 (Comprehensiveness), 56.22 (Insight), 52.76 (Instruction Following), and 53.14 (Readability).

However, I should note that I couldn't find specific information about "cellcog-max" in the search results. The leaderboard shows "Cellcog" as a single entry rather than multiple variants. If you're looking for information about a specific Cellcog variant or model configuration, you may want to check the official leaderboard directly or provide additional context about what "cellcog-max" refers to.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Raw Contents Verbatim

```
model,overall_score,comprehensiveness,insight,instruction_following,readability,citation_accuracy,effective_citations
cellcog-max,55.78,56.34,57.08,55.30,51.94,-,-
WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612,54.78,55.14,55.33,54.85,52.48,-,-
bodhi,54.07,54.15,54.60,54.41,51.87,-,-
lunon_full100_FINAL.submission,53.51,53.42,54.83,53.41,50.48,-,-
dalpha-deepresearch,53.10,52.58,52.94,53.87,53.20,-,-
sourcery,51.17,50.53,52.22,51.06,49.68,-,-
gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch,49.98,50.01,49.92,50.22,49.58,-,-
openai-deepresearch,47.84,48.05,46.69,49.29,47.62,-,-
perplexity-Research,43.05,41.78,41.27,45.31,46.03,-,-
grok-deeper-search,41.22,39.65,38.12,44.62,45.72,-,-
```

---

## Summary

The table ranks ten AI models across seven evaluation dimensions. "Cellcog-max" leads with an overall score of 55.78, performing particularly well in insight (57.08). Citation accuracy and effectiveness metrics show as unavailable across all models. Lower-ranking systems include Grok and Perplexity, scoring in the 41–43 range overall.

content.content:
def create_leaderboard_tab_gpt55():
    with gr.Tab("🏆Leaderboard"):
        gr.Markdown("""
        > **Eval Judge**: GPT-5.5 (race) + GPT-5.4-mini (fact-check)
        >
        > This tab shows results evaluated by the new judge models.
        > Data will be populated as re-evaluation completes.
        """)

        df = load_leaderboard()
        if df.empty:
            gr.Markdown("""
            ### No data yet
            Re-evaluation with GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.4-mini is pending.
            Results will appear here once available.
            """)
            return

        with gr.Row():
            with gr.Column(scale=1):
                search_box = gr.Textbox(
                    label="Model Search",
                    placeholder="Enter model name...",
                    value=""
                )
            with gr.Column(scale=2):
                category_checkboxes = gr.CheckboxGroup(
                    label="Model Categories",
                    choices=list(MODEL_CATEGORIES.keys()),
                    value=list(MODEL_CATEGORIES.keys())
                )

        initial_df = filter_data("", list(MODEL_CATEGORIES.keys()))
        if initial_df.empty:
            gr.Markdown("""
            ### No data yet
            Re-evaluation with GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.4-mini is pending.
            Results will appear here once available.
            """)
            return

        column_count = len(initial_df.columns)
=== gemini tab title ===

content.text:
Verified directly against the primary source (the Hugging Face Space repo files, fetched raw).

**What the source actually says**

`https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/raw/main/data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv` returns exactly:

```
model,overall_score,comprehensiveness,insight,instruction_following,readability,citation_accuracy,effective_citations
cellcog-max,55.78,56.34,57.08,55.30,51.94,-,-
WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612,54.78,55.14,55.33,54.85,52.48,-,-
bodhi,54.07,54.15,54.60,54.41,51.87,-,-
lunon_full100_FINAL.submission,53.51,53.42,54.83,53.41,50.48,-,-
dalpha-deepresearch,53.10,52.58,52.94,53.87,53.20,-,-
sourcery,51.17,50.53,52.22,51.06,49.68,-,-
gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch,49.98,50.01,49.92,50.22,49.58,-,-
openai-deepresearch,47.84,48.05,46.69,49.29,47.62,-,-
perplexity-Research,43.05,41.78,41.27,45.31,46.03,-,-
grok-deeper-search,41.22,39.65,38.12,44.62,45.72,-,-
```

Every number in the claim's "full GPT-5.5 board" matches, digit for digit, including cellcog-max's subscores. The cross-judge pairs also check out against `data/leaderboard.csv` (Gemini board): cellcog-max 56.67, gemini-DR 49.71, openai-DR 46.45, perplexity 40.46, grok 38.22.

**The evaluator switch is real and the GPT-5.5 board is the primary one.** `app.py` renders the header "Leaderboard tab — Race judge: GPT-5.5 | Fact-check: GPT-5.4-mini / Gemini-2.5 Eval tab — Race judge: gemini-2.5-pro", and mounts `create_leaderboard_tab_gpt55()` first as "🏆Leaderboard" while the old one is demoted to "🏆Leaderboard (Gemini-2.5 Eval)". `tabs/leaderboard_tab_gpt55.py` states "**Eval Judge**: GPT-5.5 (race) + GPT-5.4-mini (fact-check)".

**Two caveats the claim overstates**

1. The GPT-5.5 board is a *partial, in-progress* re-evaluation, not the full field. The Gemini board has 45 entries; the GPT-5.5 board has 10. Several systems that outscore cellcog-max under Gemini (qianfan 58.03, ZTE-Nebula 57.27, Link 57.08, zhipu 57.06, xiaoyi 57.00, WhaleCloud-DocChain 56.81) have **not** been re-scored under GPT-5.5. The tab itself says "Data will be populated as re-evaluation completes." So 55.78 is the highest *published* GPT-5.5 score — a valid target — but it is not a settled ceiling, and calling it "the real SOTA" is stronger than the source supports.
2. The "switched on 11 May 2026 (Gemini-2.5-Pro deprecation)" detail is not verifiable: the Space's git history is super-squashed to a single commit (2026-06-28). No primary evidence for that date or that reason.
3. The POLARIS-side arithmetic (gap 0.088 → 0.120, prior target 52.65) is internal and not checkable from this source. Note 52.65 does not appear as a row on either board.

input.reasoning:
Fetched the cited primary source raw and it matches the claim exactly. https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/raw/main/data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv returns exactly the 10 rows quoted, including cellcog-max,55.78,56.34,57.08,55.30,51.94 and the rest of the board (WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 54.78, bodhi 54.07, lunon 53.51, dalpha 53.10, sourcery 51.17, gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.98, openai-deepresearch 47.84, perplexity-Research 43.05, grok-deeper-search 41.22). The cross-judge pairs also verify against data/leaderboard.csv (Gemini board): cellcog-max 56.67, gemini-DR 49.71, openai-DR 46.45, perplexity 40.46, grok 38.22, confirming the nonlinear compress-top/lift-bottom mapping described.

The evaluator switch is confirmed structurally, not just by file existence: app.py renders "Leaderboard tab - Race judge: GPT-5.5 | Fact-check: GPT-5.4-mini" and "Gemini-2.5 Eval tab - Race judge: gemini-2.5-pro", mounts create_leaderboard_tab_gpt55() FIRST as the tab titled "Leaderboard", and demotes the old board to "Leaderboard (Gemini-2.5 Eval)". tabs/leaderboard_tab_gpt55.py declares "Eval Judge: GPT-5.5 (race) + GPT-5.4-mini (fact-check)" and reads data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv. So GPT-5.5 is the official/primary RACE judge and 55.78 is the top published score under it. Target 55.78 is correct.

Three caveats that do not overturn the verdict but qualify it:
(1) The GPT-5.5 board is an IN-PROGRESS partial re-evaluation, not the full field. The Gemini board has 45 entries; the GPT-5.5 board has 10. Systems that beat cellcog-max under Gemini (qianfan 58.03, ZTE-Nebula 57.27, Link 57.08, zhipu 57.06, xiaoyi 57.00, WhaleCloud-DocChain 56.81) have not been re-scored under GPT-5.5, and the tab itself says "Data will be populated as re-evaluation completes." So 55.78 is the highest published GPT-5.5 score and the right target today, but "the real SOTA" is stronger than the source supports -- the number can move up as re-eval completes.
(2) The specific detail "switched on 11 May 2026 (Gemini-2.5-Pro deprecation)" is UNSUPPORTED: the Space's git history is super-squashed to a single commit dated 2026-06-28, so neither the date nor the stated reason can be confirmed from the primary source. This is incidental to the claim's substance.
(3) The POLARIS-side arithmetic (gap 0.088 -> 0.120, prior target 52.65) is internal and unverifiable from this source; 52.65 appears as no row on either board.
input.corrected: Core claim stands. Two precision fixes: (a) 55.78 / cellcog-max is the top score on the GPT-5.5 board AS PUBLISHED, but that board is an incomplete re-evaluation (10 of 45 systems); six systems that outscore cellcog-max under the Gemini judge have not yet been re-scored, and the Space states "Data will be populated as re-evaluation completes" (tabs/leaderboard_tab_gpt55.py). Treat 55.78 as the current published target, not a fixed ceiling. (b) The "11 May 2026 / Gemini-2.5-Pro deprecation" switch date is unverifiable -- the HF Space commit history is super-squashed to one commit (2026-06-28T15:39:16Z, per https://huggingface.co/api/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/commits/main). The switch itself IS confirmed by app.py and tabs/leaderboard_tab_gpt55.py; only the date/reason is unsourced.
